Alko Oy Keskusvarasto.reason: Major distributor with 168-day overdue invoice #4460 (€18.5K) suggests systemic payment delay or dispute; tighten terms pending resolution.

Kesko Oyj Päivittäistavarakauppa.reason: Retail giant with €3.3K overdue 12 days; reliable payer but enforce stricter DSO to protect cash timing.

My Dear Beer B.V..reason: Export customer (€9.6K, 58 days overdue); likely DSO creep from 6–8 week export cycle; codify and monitor weekly.

Salama Shop.reason: Internal channel massively overdue (€1.3K, 74 days); own operation must pay on par with other own channels (3–5 day target).

Wittich Weine und Biere AG.reason: German distributor, €3.2K overdue 5 days; assume 6–8 week export DSO norm but enforce ceiling.

alerts.message: Escalate invoice #4460 (€18,484, 168 days overdue since 2025-10-30) to senior management today—contact decision-maker to confirm receipt, dispute status, and payment schedule within 48 hours or consider credit hold.

Total overdue (€73K, 43 invoices) is 36% of open AR and exceeds one month of fixed costs (€62.8K)—recovery of €73K is critical to avoid cash crisis by May. Implement daily overdue call blitz starting today.
Internal channel €1.3K overdue 74 days—immediate transfer from operations to receivables or halt POS supply until settled; this erodes credibility with external customers.
€9.6K overdue 58 days (invoice #5151, due 2026-02-16)—contact with specific payment date commitment; if export DSO is real, adjust future DSO to 45 days and request milestone payments.
€5.9K overdue 285 days (invoice #3837)—likely uncollectible; review for write-off or settlement offer (50–70% recovery) and update credit policy.
44 open POs (€80K) + €121K open AR = €201K gross exposure on ~€202K monthly budget revenue; verify PO payment terms do not compress cash cycle further—defer non-critical orders until AR improves.
€25K/month contract ends September 2026 (4.5 months away)—model revenue impact and identify replacement customers to protect €150K pipeline before gap.
€4.5K overdue 580 days (invoice #14, due 2024-08-14)—write off or escalate to collections; carrying dead debt wastes AR resources.

insights.weekly_cash_risk: €73K overdue AR (36% of open book) plus €80K open POs creates 2–3 week cash runway risk if major debtors (Alko €18.5K, My Dear Beer €9.6K) do not remit by end of month, threatening €62.8K fixed cost coverage in May.
insights.top_action: Call Alko Oy Keskusvarasto decision-maker at 09:00 CET today to confirm payment status of invoice #4460 (€18.5K, 168 days overdue); escalate to your CFO if debtor claims non-receipt or disputes—this single invoice is 25% of overdue AR and cannot slip further.
pattern_insights: Export customers (My Dear Beer, Wittich, Gig Linden) cluster in 5–12 day overdue range on recently due items, suggesting 6–8 week DSO is structural; codify and separate export DSO from domestic (25 days) to improve forecasting.

Three invoices >100 days overdue (Alko €18.5K, OlutSatama €5.9K, Wet Goods €4.5K = €28.8K or 39% of overdue bucket) are likely uncollectible or blocked by disputes; triage immediately for write-off vs. recovery effort to free AR team capacity.
insights.wc_note: At 62% YoY growth with €121K open AR + €80K open POs against ~€39K monthly sales run rate, working capital is severely stretched; cash DSO of 50–60 days (implied by export cycle) conflicts with 44-day PO payment cycle, creating 2–4 week negative gap—urgent AR recovery and PO deferral required to sustain growth without external financing.
